meta.title: Dopamine, Serotonin, and Norepinephrine Interactions in Shaping Animal Behavior
meta.topic: How dopamine, serotonin, and norepinephrine interact and function together to shape animal behavior, including cellular mechanisms underlying these interactions

papers.title: Ultrafast neuronal imaging of dopamine dynamics with designed genetically encoded sensors
key_claims: dLight1 enables ultrafast optical recording of dopamine dynamics in behaving animals

papers.title: Reward and Aversion
key_claims: Reward and aversion processing involve overlapping but distinct neural circuits

papers.title: Association between depressive behavior and absence of serotonin-dopamine interaction in the nucleus accumbens
key_claims: Inability of serotonin to stimulate dopamine release in NAc may be essential factor in depression onset

papers.title: Norepinephrine versus dopamine and their interaction in modulating synaptic function in the prefrontal cortex
key_claims: Both catecholamines converge on PFC neurons with opposing and cooperative effects

papers.title: An Update on the Role of Serotonin and its Interplay with Dopamine for Reward
key_claims: Additive effect of serotonin and DA conveys significant reward-related information that is subjectively euphorizing

papers.title: Modulating the Neuromodulators: Dopamine, Serotonin, and the Endocannabinoid System
key_claims: Endocannabinoids modulate both DA and 5-HT systems forming a tripartite neuromodulatory network

papers.title: Serotonin/dopamine interaction: Electrophysiological and neurochemical evidence
key_claims: 5-HT modulates DA neuron activity through multiple receptor subtypes with region-specific effects

papers.title: Monoamines: Dopamine, Norepinephrine, and Serotonin, Beyond Modulation, Switches That Alter the State of Target Networks
key_claims: Monoamines function as switches that alter the state of target networks rather than simple modulators

papers.title: Therapeutic Potential of 5-HT2C Receptor Agonists for Addictive Disorders
key_claims: 5-HT2C receptors modulate motivation through regulation of DA release in striatum

papers.title: Transient and sustained effects of dopamine and serotonin signaling in learning
key_claims: Dopamine effects on plasticity range from milliseconds (phasic) to days (sustained)

papers.title: Reward and aversion processing by input-defined parallel nucleus accumbens circuits
key_claims: NAc neurons are organized by their inputs: BLA input neurons promote positive reinforcement, PVT input neurons mediate aversion

papers.title: Anhedonia in Depression and Schizophrenia: Brain Reward and Aversion Circuits
key_claims: Depression shows impaired anticipatory pleasure (frontostriatal); schizophrenia shows deficits in reward value representation (mesocortical)

papers.title: Striatal Serotonin Release Signals Reward Value
key_claims: 5-HT in dorsomedial striatum tracks subjective reward value

papers.title: Review: Dopamine, Serotonin, and the Translational Neuroscience of Aggression in Children and Adolescents
key_claims: DA acts as behavioral accelerator, 5-HT as brake; imbalance leads to impulsivity and aggression

papers.title: Sex specific effects of endocrine-disrupting chemicals on brain monoamines and cognitive function
key_claims: Endocrine-disrupting chemicals have sex-specific effects on monoamine systems and cognition

papers.title: Dopamine and serotonin in human substantia nigra track social context and value signals during economic exchange
papers.notes: Auto-discovered. Abstract: Dopamine and serotonin are hypothesized to guide social behaviours. In humans, however, we have not yet been able to study neuromodulator dynamics as social interaction unfolds. Here, we obtained subs...

update_history.description: Initial literature review with 15 papers from Semantic Scholar search
Converted to living review format for continuous updates
Added 1 new papers from Semantic Scholar search
